title: entada leptostachya
intro: Entada leptostachya, also known as the "Fish Poison Tree" or "Horse's Hoof", is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, the roots and seeds of this plant have been used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Entada leptostachya include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of Entada leptostachya may include decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which can be used to create traditional remedies and supplements.
